title: How to request a waiver for citizenship requirements - Canada.ca
content:
Waiver for citizenship requirements

## How to request a waiver
Answer the following questions to
- get the right forms and documents for your situation
- find out how to submit them
- You can send the documents with your application or after you’ve already applied. If you’ve already applied, you can only submit them if we haven’t made a decision on your application yet (approval or refusal)
- You can send the documents with your application or after you’ve already applied.
- If you’ve already applied, you can only submit them if we haven’t made a decision on your application yet (approval or refusal)
An applicant or their helper (such as a representative, legal guardian or caregiver) may answer the questions.

## Waiver of the oath of citizenship and the test or language requirements
You told us that the applicant
- is an adult (18 or above)
- needs a waiver for the oath of citizenship test or language requirement (or both)
- test or language requirement (or both)
- has a mental disability (such as an intellectual or a developmental disability)
- has a representative who’s also a guardian
- Prepare the forms and documents

### Prepare the forms and documents
Only a health care professional licensed to practice in Canada can fill out the form.
Download the Medical Opinion Form for Citizenship Waivers [CIT 0547] (opens in a new tab)
The completed form needs to
- describe the mental disability (such as an intellectual or a developmental disability)
- if applicable , explain any severe medical condition that has lasted (or may last) for at least 1 year For example, if the medical condition caused or coexists with the mental disability.
- For example, if the medical condition caused or coexists with the mental disability.
Fill out this form to request a waiver for the citizenship test or language requirement (or both).
Download the Waiver Request Form [CIT 0116] (opens in a new tab)
If applicable, include
- additional pages for your answers (if you don’t have enough space in the form)
- for questions 8 and 9: a class attendance report or a letter from a learning centre or your teacher
- a class attendance report or
- a letter from a learning centre or your teacher
A representative must complete the Use of a Representative Form (opens in a new tab) .
You must provide guardianship documents if you’re either
- the legal guardian or caregiver of an adult (18 and above) applicant or
- the guardian of a minor between 14 and 17 years old who doesn’t have custody of the minor can’t legally act on their behalf
- doesn’t have custody of the minor
- can’t legally act on their behalf
Prepare and submit 1 of these documents to prove your relationship with the applicant:
- a power of attorney document
- an affidavit (sworn statement) for the guardianship How to complete your affidavit
- How to complete your affidavit
How to complete your affidavit
How to prepare document
The document must be
- clear and easy to read
- in English or French
If it’s not in English or French, provide
- a certified translation (opens in a new tab) and
- an affidavit (sworn statement) for the translation (opens in a new tab)
An affidavit is a sworn statement. As the applicants’ guardian, you can submit an affidavit if you don’t have a power of attorney document or a court order.
To complete your affidavit:
Start your affidavit with the following statement:
[Your name] of the [city or town] in the [province or territory] affirm that
I am the [your relationship with the applicant] of the [applicant’s name] born on [applicant’s date of birth] in [applicant’s place of birth], who is applying (or applied for) Canadian citizenship on [date the application was signed].
In the affidavit, tell us
- any legal authority you have on the applicant
- why you’re acting on behalf of the applicant
- whether you’re acting in the best interest of the applicant
- the applicant’s needs and wishes (for example, if they want to take the citizenship test or oath in person)
- if the applicant’s medical information was provided to us (Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada)
- the application number, if you have it You’ll only get this number if you’ve already submitted the application and receive the acknowledgement of receipt (AOR) letter.
- You’ll only get this number if you’ve already submitted the application and receive the acknowledgement of receipt (AOR) letter.
You need to write the affidavit in
You (the guardian) must sign the affidavit in the presence of an authorized person, like
- a commissioner of oaths
- a commissioner of taking affidavits
If you can, include a signed statement from another person to confirm your guardian or caregiver relationship with the applicant. This supporting third party statement can be written by
You may send other documents that can help us review your request, like
- a medical test result or report
- an audiogram and an attestation from an audiologist (for a hearing impairment)

### Submit the forms and documents
Get the instructions to submit the forms and documents:
Check if you can apply online (opens in a new tab) . If you’re not eligible to apply online, apply on paper (opens in a new tab) .
Before you submit your application, upload the completed forms and any supporting documents in the “Other documents” section of your online account.
Include the completed forms and any supporting documents in your mailed paper application (opens in a new tab)
You can request a waiver after you’ve submitted your application (if you didn’t request it when you applied).
To send your waiver forms and documents, follow these steps:
- Wait until you receive an invitation to a citizenship event (like the citizenship test, ceremony or interview).
- Follow the instructions in the invitation.
